markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 73 The voices of generations past speak to you, and you hear their words. You might resent the constant interruption, or you might revere the spirits of those who came before. Perhaps you hail from a culture with strong ancestral traditions, such as the Shoanti Skoan-Quah (Skull Clan), a traditional dwarven community that worships [Torag's](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) [family pantheon](/Deities.aspx?ID=196), or a group that reveres [Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=6) or [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14). You might commune with powerful psychopomps who shepherd souls to the afterlife, with the River of Souls itself, or with those spirits who have become trapped outside of the great cycle of spiritual rebirth. You learn from their whispers and the fragments of their memories, but opening your mind to their knowledge and experience also allows them to meddle in your worldly affairs. **Mystery Benefit** The whispers of your ancestors have bestowed additional knowledge upon you. You gain an additional ancestry feat at 1st level and another additional ancestry feat at 11th level. These feats can't be ones that grant physiological effects, such as additional senses or unarmed attacks. **Trained Skill** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) **Granted Cantrip** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) **Initial Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=744) **Advanced Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Defense](/Spells.aspx?ID=742) **Greater Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=743) **Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7), [Family](/Domains.aspx?ID=11) Curse of Ancestral Meddling The ancestral spirits you commune with haunt you and meddle with your belongings and actions, either out of a well-intentioned (but ultimately detrimental) attempt to assist you, as punishment for your audacity in circumventing the traditional means of achieving divine power, for their own amusement, or a mixture of the above. Your hair, clothing, and belongings constantly shift and stir, seemingly of their own volition. **Minor Curse** One of your ancestors becomes predominant in their meddling. The first time you gain this effect each day, roll 1d4 on Table 2–4: Ancestral Influence to determine which type of ancestor becomes predominant. This predominant ancestor guides you to use their preferred type of action. When you try to use one of the types of actions listed for the other ancestors, you must succeed at a DC 4 flat check. On a failure, you spend the action but gain no effect (though you don't lose the spell if you were Casting a Spell). If your action would take 1 minute or longer, it's long enough that you can overcome the meddling, and you don't need to attempt a flat check. During combat or another tense, round-by-round encounter, other ancestors wrestle for control of your actions. When you roll initiative, and then at the end of each of your turns during the encounter, roll 1d4 and change your predominant ancestor appropriately. Once the encounter ends, you return to the influence of the ancestor you first rolled for the day. **Moderate Curse** The flat check DC to use other actions is 6, and you get the moderate curse benefit listed for your predominant ancestor in Table 2–4. **Major Curse** The flat check DC is 8, and you get the major curse benefit listed for your predominant ancestor instead of the moderate curse benefit. ## Table 2-4: Ancestral Influence
**1d4****Ancestor****Preferred Action****Moderate Curse Benefit****Major Curse Benefit**
1MartialStrikesYour Strikes gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and a +2 status bonus to damage.Your Strikes gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and a +6 status bonus to damage.
2SkillfulPerception and Skill actionsYou gain a +1 status bonus to skill checks and Perception checksYou gain a +2 status bonus to skill checks and Perception checks.
3SpellcastingCast a SpellNon-cantrip spells without a duration gain a status bonus to their damage and healing equal to the spell's levelNon-cantrip spells without a duration gain a status bonus to their damage and healing equal to the spell's level + 3
4You choose which ancestor guides your actions.
name: Ancestors

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Battle](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 74

Warlike forces fill you with physical might and tactical knowledge, aiming to have you uphold the glory of combat, fight to improve the world, prepare against the necessity of conflict, or endure the inevitability of war. You might draw upon deities such as [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9), [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=15), the Horseman of War [Szuriel](/Deities.aspx?ID=136), the Queen of the Night [Eiseth](/Deities.aspx?ID=169), the Vudrani god [Diomazul](/Deities.aspx?ID=190), and others, or you might find power in the unending conflict between the armies of [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17) and [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18), the Elemental Planes, the Outer Gods, or even the collective spirits of those who fought in wars over the ages.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** You are no stranger to the trappings of warfare. You are trained in medium and heavy armor. At 13th level, if you gain the light armor expertise class feature, you also gain expert proficiency in these armors.

 Choose one weapon group that embodies your mystery. You are trained in all martial weapons of that group. At 11th level, if you gain the weapon expertise class feature, you also gain expert proficiency for martial weapons in your chosen group, and you become trained in advanced weapons in that group.

**Trained Skill**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

**Granted Cantrip** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Call to Arms](/Spells.aspx?ID=748)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Battlefield Persistence](/Spells.aspx?ID=746)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Heroic Feat](/Spells.aspx?ID=754)

**Related Domains** [Might](/Domains.aspx?ID=20), [Zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=37)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of the Hero's Burden</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You thrive while adrenaline flows and your life depends on your might alone, but holding the collective battle prowess of the ages within you leaves your body weakened after a fight. You smell faintly of steel and blood no matter how you try to remove or mask the scent, you appear more muscular than you actually are, and you hear the faint clash and clamor of battle in the distance at all times.

**Minor Curse** Your body languishes when you aren't bringing harm to your foes. You take a –2 status penalty to AC and saving throws, but each time you make a Strike, you can suspend these penalties until the start of your next turn.

**Moderate Curse** The strain of conflict wears upon your body, even though you gain vitality from it. Making a Strike reduces the penalty from your minor curse to –1 rather than suspending it entirely. You gain a +2 status bonus to weapon and unarmed damage rolls. You also gain [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=796) equal to half your level while in a non-trivial combat encounter.

**Major Curse** Your body proves capable of truly heroic feats, but doing so interferes with your mental focus. Your moderate curse's fast healing is equal to your level, and its damage bonus is +6. You gain a +1 status bonus to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, but you are [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37).
name: Battle

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 75

Your mystery imparts an understanding of death and undeath in all their macabre complexity. You might have had a brush with death yourself—maybe even dying and returning to life—or carry the touch of undeath in your blood. If you commune with deities, you might speak with guardians of death like [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) and the [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) ushers (the most powerful among creatures that guide souls through the afterlife); a bringer of death like the Mantis God [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=29) or the Horseman of the Apocalypse [Charon](/Deities.aspx?ID=135); or a herald of undeath such as [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** You can cleverly leverage the subtleties of life and death to your benefit. Each day during your preparations, you can choose to align yourself in the confluence of positive and negative energy so as to gain [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), which means you are harmed by positive damage and healed by negative effects as if you were undead. If you already have negative healing, instead the DC of your recovery checks is equal to 9 + your current [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value.

**Trained Skill**
[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

**Granted Cantrip** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Soul Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=760)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Armor of Bones](/Spells.aspx?ID=745)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Claim Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=749)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7), [Undeath](/Domains.aspx?ID=34)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Living Death</title>

<traits>

</traits>

Your body is slowly decaying even though you are alive, and using your powers furthers this unnatural living death. You carry a touch of the grave about you, manifesting as bloodless pallor, a faint smell of earth, or deathly cold skin.

**Minor Curse** Bones protrude through your skin and your flesh rots, combining with a stench of decay and an unearthly chill. Non-magical effects that would restore your Hit Points only grant you half as many HP as normal.

**Moderate Curse** Your dead flesh continues to rot, affecting your health. You become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and this drained condition is cumulative with any other drained conditions you gain. This drained condition is removed only when you [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71); effects that normally remove the drained condition reduce your drained condition to drained 1 instead. In addition, you gain resistance to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) equal to half your level. You gain a +4 status bonus to saves against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), poisons, and [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, and if you roll a success on such a save, you get a critical success instead.

**Major Curse** You stand just steps from the grave. You become [wounded 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42). This wounded condition is removed only when you Refocus; effects that normally remove the wounded condition reduce your wounded condition to wounded 1 instead. When you would attempt a recovery check, you can choose to get an automatic success instead of rolling, and if you roll a critical failure on a save against a disease, poison, or death effect, you get a failure instead.
name: Bones

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Cosmos](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 75

Celestial bodies great and small exert influence on you, giving you sublime cosmic power. Perhaps you see the glittering stars as a divine blessing, or perhaps you feel drawn to the infinitely dark spaces between. You might uphold deities like [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), or the deific lovers [Shizuru](/Deities.aspx?ID=47) and [Tsukiyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=48) who represent the sun and the moon—or you might draw power from dark entities from beyond the stars, like certain Outer Gods, or destructive gods of the night like [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=20) or the rat goddess [Lao Shu Po](/Deities.aspx?ID=180).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** Your body is as much an astronomical one as it is physical. You gain resistance equal to 2 + half your level against all physical damage.

**Trained Skill**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Granted Cantrip** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Spray of Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=761)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Interstellar Void](/Spells.aspx?ID=756)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Moonlight Bridge](/Spells.aspx?ID=759)

**Related Domains** [Darkness](/Domains.aspx?ID=6), [Moon](/Domains.aspx?ID=21)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of the Sky's Call</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You have your head in the clouds—and not just figuratively. Your body is drawn toward the heavens, making you lighter and less substantial than you should be. Your eyes glow with starry light, and your hair and clothing float and drift around you.

**Minor Curse** Your unnatural lightness makes it hard to keep your footing and interact with other physical objects. You are [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and take a –2 penalty to saves and DCs against [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), and other forms of [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).

**Moderate Curse** Your body is drawn further skyward. You are enfeebled 2, and the penalty from your minor curse increases to –3. You are treated as one size smaller for wind effects. You gain a +2 status bonus against [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) attempts, you only take half as much damage from falls, and you gain the effects of the [Powerful Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=820) and [Quick Jump](/Feats.aspx?ID=825) skill feats. You weigh only half as much, and your Bulk, should someone need to carry you, is also half as much.

**Major Curse** As your body rises, you float just above the ground beneath you. You become enfeebled 4, and the penalty from your minor curse increases to –4. You can walk on liquids as if they were solid; you gain the effects of the [Cloud Jump](/Feats.aspx?ID=767) skill feat; and you don't leave tracks, trigger weight-sensitive pressure plates, or otherwise connect with the ground below you.
name: Cosmos

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Flames](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 76

Fire lives at the center of the world, the center of the sun, and the center of civilization. You might revere this elemental force, siphon power from the [Elemental Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6), or venerate a collection of deities such as [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), the Tian goddess of disasters and volcanoes [Lady Nanbyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=179), or the elemental lord of fire [Ymeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=108).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** Like fire itself, you flicker and dance, avoiding effects that would quench your vitality. You have expert proficiency in Reflex saves. At 13th level, if you gain the lightning reflexes class feature, your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to master, and when you roll a success on a Reflex save, you get a critical success instead.

**Trained Skill**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1)

**Granted Cantrip** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Incendiary Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=755)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Whirling Flames](/Spells.aspx?ID=766)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Flaming Fusillade](/Spells.aspx?ID=753)

**Related Domains** [Fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=13), [Sun](/Domains.aspx?ID=29)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Engulfing Flames</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You see flames and smoke wherever you look. These flames might be imagined, or they might be a preternatural glimpse of the metaphorical fires that empower the entire multiverse—but you always see them. Fires flare noticeably (though not dangerously) in your presence, you occasionally smoke slightly, and your body is almost painfully hot to the touch.

**Minor Curse** The smoke, heat, and crackling flames of your curse fill your vision and all your other senses. Creatures further than 30 feet are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you. You can't benefit from effects that would allow you to ignore or mitigate this concealment, as normal for effects of an oracular curse.

**Moderate Curse** Smoke and flickering visions of flame fill your senses more completely, and harmless flickers of obscuring flames also fill your space. You are concealed from other creatures, though as the other creatures aren't cursed themselves, they can benefit from effects that would allow them to ignore or mitigate the concealed condition, as normal. All other creatures and objects are concealed from you regardless of distance; however, when casting a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) spell, you automatically succeed at the flat check for this concealed condition for targets within 30 feet.

 All your senses become imprecise beyond 30 feet, meaning everything past 30 feet that you'd normally be able to see is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from you.

**Major Curse** The flames surrounding you are no longer simply visions. An aura of fire surrounds you in a 10-foot emanation, dealing 4d6 fire damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to all other creatures in the aura at the end of each of your turns. You lose 1d6 Hit Points at the end of your turn each round, with no save; if you have a weakness to fire, increase the number of HP you lose by that weakness.

 You can suppress your aura until the start of your next turn by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to diminish the flames, causing neither you nor anyone in the aura to take damage. While [Refocusing](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) to reduce your curse, you are continually diminishing the flames, so you don't lose HP. The flames subside if you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), but they resume when you wake up unless you rested for long enough to reset your curse. As usual for oracular curses, you can't mitigate or reduce the lost Hit Points in any way, though you can still heal the lost HP normally after the fact.
name: Flames

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Life](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 77

The never-ending flow of positive energy within living beings is palpable to you. You might uphold the sanctity of life, or perhaps you seek to undermine it. You might draw power from the collective vitality of the world's living creatures, hold some connection to the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3), or revere a collection of deities including [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10), [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), and the god of medicine [Qi Zhong](/Deities.aspx?ID=182).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** Your body is a deep reservoir of life energy. At each level, you gain Hit Points equal to 10 + your Constitution modifier from the oracle class, instead of 8 + your Constitution modifier.

**Trained Skill**
[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

**Granted Cantrip** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Life Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=758)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Delay Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=751)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Life-Giving Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=757)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=7), [Healing](/Domains.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Outpouring Life</title>

<traits>

</traits>

Life energy flows outward from you and connects you to all living things, but you expend your vital essence to do so. Your presence comforts the ill and injured, causes scars to fade slightly, spurs new growth in plants, and otherwise infuses your surroundings with vitality.

**Minor Curse** As your life force seeps outward, it becomes more difficult to keep your body functioning. Effects that restore Hit Points to you take a status penalty equal to half your level (minimum 1) to the number of HP you recover.

**Moderate Curse** The flow of life energy away from you can't be reversed. In addition to the effects of your minor curse, you can't be healed by magical effects originating from other creatures. However, if you are [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), magical effects can restore you to 1 HP (but no higher). You are affected normally by healing [elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8), [potions](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=18), and other items. When you cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) and all your targets are living creatures, you roll d12s instead of d8s for the amount of healing.

 Whenever you finish casting a non-cantrip spell, you restore Hit Points equal to the spell level to your choice of either one target of the spell or the creature nearest to you. You can't heal yourself in this way. This healing has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), and [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) traits, as well as the tradition trait of the spell.

**Major Curse** Life energy tears through you to empower your magic. Some of your spells carry positive energy with them. Each time you use a spell slot to cast a 5th-level or higher spell that takes 2 or more actions to cast, you disperse positive energy in a 30-foot burst with the effects of a 3-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell with a level 4 lower than that of the spell you cast. This healing occurs immediately after you finish Casting the Spell. You don't benefit from this healing. Instead, you lose double the number of Hit Points rolled for the _heal_ spell.
name: Life

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Lore](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 77

Knowledge and information come freely to you. You might use this lore to gain power or perhaps to understand the divine mysteries of the multiverse. You might have a conduit to the fabled Akashic Record, maintain a subtle telepathic connection to the collective subconscious of all living creatures, or follow in the footsteps of deities such as [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=1), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10), [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12), Irori's scholarly nephew [Gruhastha](/Deities.aspx?ID=39), the fey triune goddess of fate [Magdh](/Deities.aspx?ID=97), or the lawful aeon god-figure known as the [Monad](/Deities.aspx?ID=141).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** You hold more mystical knowledge within you than most. You have one additional spell in your repertoire of each level you can cast.

**Trained Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)
and one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill of your choice.

**Granted Cantrip** [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Brain Drain](/Spells.aspx?ID=747)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Access Lore](/Spells.aspx?ID=741)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Dread Secret](/Spells.aspx?ID=752)

**Related Domains** [Knowledge](/Domains.aspx?ID=17), [Truth](/Domains.aspx?ID=32)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Torrential Knowledge</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You have a link to true divine knowledge, but your mortal mind struggles to process and act on what you know. Loose materials around you, such as dust, grains of rice, and droplets of water, slowly shift to form strange runes or faint indecipherable writing, and you sometimes speak unintelligible truths or statements in unknown languages without realizing it.

**Minor Curse** You take a –4 penalty to initiative, as trying to process a surge of information about what's happening around you slows your ability to respond to it.

**Moderate Curse** The flow of information through your mind grows. The attention you divert to process this huge influx of information means you are always flat-footed. At the start of each of your turns, you automatically make one check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) as a free action. This uses a skill of your choice that has the Recall Knowledge action, using a result equal to 10 + your proficiency bonus in that skill (with no other bonuses, penalties, or modifiers).

**Major Curse** The massive flow of information clouding your mind overwhelms your ability to communicate with others. You can understand all languages, but you can't speak, use [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) effects, or otherwise communicate with your allies. If you Cast a Spell with a verbal component, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the spell is lost. You gain a +4 status bonus on saving throws against linguistic effects.
name: Lore

<additional-info>

You gain access to unparalleled, overwhelming knowledge.
</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Tempest](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 78

The fury of the wind and waves pounds in your heart, whether your power flows from natural storms, a conduit to the elemental [Planes of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) and [Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7), or through reverence of deities such as [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=8), the tengu god of storms [Hei Feng](/Deities.aspx?ID=40), the demon lord [Dagon](/Deities.aspx?ID=75), or the elemental lords of air and water.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** You can see perfectly through wind and water, and you send electric charges through both air and water. You never take penalties to Perception from wind, rain, fog, or other precipitation, or from looking through water or being underwater, and such conditions don't cause anything to be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you.

 When you deal physical damage with a non-cantrip [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) spell, you deal an additional 1 electricity damage per spell level.

**Trained Skill**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Granted Cantrip** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Tempest Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=763)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Thunderburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=764)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Tempest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=762)

**Related Domains** [Air](/Domains.aspx?ID=2), [Water](/Domains.aspx?ID=35)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of the Perpetual Storm</title>

<traits>

</traits>

You are the center of your own tiny tempest, ever surrounded by wind and rain that worsens the more you tap into your elemental powers. Even when you are calm and at rest, your hair and clothing are blown about by gentle winds, you are slightly damp, and your touch often comes with a static shock.

**Minor Curse** An aura of a whirling storm whips up in a 5-foot emanation around you and in your space. The aura puts out small non-magical fires 1 round after they're lit or brought into your aura.

 Electrical energy builds up within your storm, turning you into a lightning rod; you gain weakness 2 to electricity, and [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) spells or effects that have additional effects for a creature wearing or holding metal treat you as though you were wearing metal.

**Moderate Curse** Your minor curse's aura expands to a 10- foot emanation and carries rain on the winds. The whirling winds impose a –2 circumstance penalty on ranged attack rolls using physical ammunition that target you or originate from you. Your weakness to electricity increases to 5 or half your level, whichever is greater, but torrential rain grants you an equal amount of fire resistance.

**Major Curse** Your minor curse's aura expands to a 15-foot emanation and grows stronger. High winds blowing outward in the aura impede creatures' progress. The aura is difficult terrain for Large and smaller creatures on the ground, and for Huge or smaller flying creatures. You aren't affected by this difficult terrain.

 The electricity arcing through you becomes dangerous. Any creature that touches you or damages you with an unarmed melee attack or non-reach melee weapon takes 1d6 electricity damage. As normal, this doesn't affect creatures you touch.
name: Tempest

<additional-info>
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 78
</additional-info>

<summary>
Wind, waves, and storms rage at your beck and call.
</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Time](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=9)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 188

Whether by accident, fate, or purposeful meddling, you exist slightly outside of time. While initially unmoored by moments, the countless march of milliseconds builds up to drift you further and further out of sync with your natural timeline, often in contradictory directions. You might view this as a blessing, perhaps as a sign you're somehow beyond mortality or you can't be contained within reality's limitations, or you might view it as a cruel curse that causes your own life to slip by adrift even as you gain power over time. Many oracles of time are without faith and either come to rely upon their own instincts to survive or else turn to strange rituals and forbidden magic in an attempt to break free of time's grasp. Those oracles who worship deities most often worship [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), goddess of fate who sees all possible futures for all who ever lived, or [Alseta](/Deities.aspx?ID=30), goddess of transitions and the passage through time.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** Time passes more swiftly for you than it does for those around you, enabling you to cover more distance in what others perceive as the same amount of time. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed. At 6th level, this increases to a +15-foot status bonus; at 10th level, this increases to a +20-foot status bonus; at 14th level, this increases to a +25-foot status bonus; and at 18th level, it increases to a +30-foot status bonus. Your disjointed movement makes it harder for creatures to hit you as you move. You gain a +1 status bonus to AC against attacks made against you from reactions or free actions while you're moving.

**Trained Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Granted Cantrip** [Time Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1193)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Temporal Distortion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1195)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Time Skip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1196)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Manifold Lives](/Spells.aspx?ID=1197)

**Related Domains** [Fate](/Domains.aspx?ID=12), [Time](/Domains.aspx?ID=54)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Turbulent Moments</title>

<traits>

</traits>

Time passes differently for you than everyone else. You experience fluxes that might manifest as minor day-to-day changes in your apparent age, such as a gray lock of hair or a disappearing wrinkle, or as occasional ghostly temporal afterimages that slightly trail your movement.

**Minor Curse** As you draw upon your mystery, time's passage becomes more disjointed, aging, regressing, or warping you out of sync. You become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13). In addition, you take a –2 penalty to saving throws against effects that would make you [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).

**Moderate Curse** Time distorts further, increasing your muscular atrophy, age regression, or warping while making effects pass you by at an increased rate. The value of your enfeebled condition increases to 2 and the penalty from your minor curse increases to –3. Your status bonus to AC against attacks made against you from reactions or free actions while you're moving increases to +2. In addition, spells affecting you last less time; their duration is reduced by half, to a minimum of 1 round. This doesn't reduce the spell's duration for other targets affected by the same spell. Finally, all non-permanent conditions affecting you that have a duration measured in rounds, minutes, or hours have their duration reduced by half, to a minimum of 1 round. Any afflictions (such as [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), and [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=38)) affecting you have their maximum duration, onset time, and time for each stage reduced by half. Spells, conditions, and afflictions that last “until your next daily preparations” last for 12 hours or until your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. This doesn't change the duration you're temporarily immune, the rate at which you can use abilities that have a frequency, or any other features based on the passage of time.

**Major Curse** You become so out of sync with time that it's hard for you to act, but when you do act, you do so with incredible speed. You become slowed 1, the value of your enfeebled condition increases to 3, and the penalty from your minor curse increases to –4. Time passes so much faster for you that your steps appear as a blur of motion to others. You gain a +4 status bonus to initiative rolls. Whenever you move, you're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while you're moving and until the start of your next turn. As normal for concealment where your location is still obvious, you can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63). Your status bonus to AC against attacks made against you from reactions or free actions while you're moving increases to +4.
name: Time

<additional-info>

</additional-info>

</summary>

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Limited">[Ash](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=10)</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 80

You see all things in the world as little more than fleeting and temporary, waiting to be purified into their base essence: the ash left behind after a burning fire. While you understand fire is a necessary part of this process, you see fire mostly as a tool to achieve final purity, not a goal. You have much in common with oracles with the [flames mystery](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=5), but you may consider them to be short-sighted, or at best simply lacking in understanding of the truths that their burning fires impart.

 You might see ash as the only true representation of the impermanent nature of life and its pointless existence. Alternatively, you may see the benefit ash can have on soils and farmlands as part of a larger cycle of death and rebirth. Perhaps you see the [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=330), with its life cycle of being reduced to ash and reborn, as a sacred creature that has achieved a higher state of being and worthy of imitation (or maybe you see it as a tragic creature that can't ever reach the goal of perfection). If you follow a deity, you might follow gods of fire like [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), those who burn impurities to ash such as [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=33) or [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18), or those who strive to burn all things to ash, such as the archdevil [Moloch](/Deities.aspx?ID=70) or the demon lord Flauros.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Benefit** Your flesh flows around physical threats like a cloud of ash. You gain resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) against all physical damage and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves and DCs against [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), and [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).

**Trained Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Granted Cantrip** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Ashen Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1242)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Incendiary Ashes](/Spells.aspx?ID=1243)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Ash Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1241)

**Related Domains** [Destruction](/Domains.aspx?ID=8), [Fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Limited">Curse of Creeping Ashes</title>

<traits>

</traits>

Your body is slowly being consumed by the fires of your internal power, purifying you with each passing day. You are occasionally wracked with dry, wheezing coughs, and wherever you go you leave behind a fine trace of ash that falls from your body.

**Minor Curse** The smoldering furnace of power within you begins to feed on your body and generate ash, which you inhale with every breath and leave behind with every step, impeding your footing. You take a –1 penalty on Constitution-based checks, such as Fortitude saves, and your Speed is reduced by 10 feet.

**Moderate Curse** The ash you produce intensifies and fills the area around you, obscuring details and making the ground challenging to navigate. You are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). An aura of ash surrounds you in a 10-foot emanation. You and creatures in the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). If you do not move for 1 round, heaps of ashes settle in the aura, creating difficult terrain that lasts until you move (when the ashes blow away).

**Major Curse** You and nearby creatures collapse into ash with the slightest flame. Within your moderate curse's aura, you and all creatures gain weakness to fire equal to half your level. If a creature other than you in this aura is killed by fire damage, its body collapses into ash. Objects and structures in your aura halve their Hardness, and any parts of the object or structures in your aura that are reduced to 0 Hit Points collapse into ash.
name: Ash

<additional-info>
**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 80

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Ancestors](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=12)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 135

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Any deities or character options which grant the _dreaming potential_ spell instead grant _illusory scene_._

The voices of generations past speak to you, and you hear their words. You might resent the constant interruption, or you might revere the spirits of those who came before. Perhaps you hail from a culture with strong ancestral traditions, such as the Shoanti Skoan-Quah (Skull Clan), a traditional [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) community that worships [Torag’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=294) family pantheon, or a group that reveres [Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=282) or [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290). You might commune with powerful [psychopomps](/Traits.aspx?ID=679) who shepherd souls to the afterlife, with the [River of Souls](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) itself, or with those spirits who have become trapped outside of the great cycle of spiritual rebirth. You learn from their whispers and the fragments of their memories, but opening your mind to their knowledge and experience also allows them to meddle in your worldly affairs.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549)
- **1st** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)
- **2nd** [Ghostly Carrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=1543)
- **5th** [Dreaming Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=1504)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=2066)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Defense](/Spells.aspx?ID=2067)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Ancestral Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=2068)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=70), [Duty](/Domains.aspx?ID=45), [Family](/Domains.aspx?ID=74), [Soul](/Domains.aspx?ID=51)

**Oracle Feat** [Whispers of Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=6057)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Ancestral Meddling</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
</traits>

The ancestral spirits you commune with haunt you and meddle with your belongings and actions, either out of a wellintentioned (but ultimately detrimental) attempt to assist you, as punishment for your audacity in circumventing the traditional means of achieving divine power, for their own amusement, or a mixture of the above. When you have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) condition, you are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) with a value equal to your cursebound value as the spirits of your ancestors temporarily possess you and vie for control in your mind, hindering your movements.
name: Ancestors

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Battle](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=13)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 135

Warlike forces fill you with physical might and tactical knowledge, aiming to have you uphold the glory of combat, fight to improve the world, prepare against the necessity of conflict, or endure the inevitability of war. You might draw upon deities such as [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=283), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=291), the [Horseman of War Szuriel](/Deities.aspx?ID=136), the [Queen of the Night Eiseth](/Deities.aspx?ID=169), the [Vudrani god Diomazul](/Deities.aspx?ID=190), and others, or you might find power in the unending conflict between the armies of [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) and [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), [the Elemental Planes](/Planes.aspx?Category=1), [the Outer Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13), or even the collective spirits of those who fought in wars over the ages.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
- **1st** [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
- **2nd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717)
- **4th** [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Weapon Trance](/Spells.aspx?ID=2069)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Battlefield Persistence](/Spells.aspx?ID=2070)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Revel in Retribution](/Spells.aspx?ID=2071)

**Related Domains** [Destruction](/Domains.aspx?ID=71), [Might](/Domains.aspx?ID=83), [Protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=90), [Zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=100)

**Oracle Feat** [Oracular Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=6056)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of the Mortal Warrior</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

You thrive in the thick of battle, but your mystery’s sheer focus on the physical and material leaves your soul weak against the tricks of spellcraft. You smell faintly of steel and blood no matter how you try to remove or mask the scent, you appear more imposing and muscular than you actually are, and you hear the faint clash and clamor of battle in the distance at all times.

**Cursebound 1** Spells have an easier time wounding you. You gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 to any damage dealt by a spell. Any [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) or [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) you have to spells is suppressed. This applies only to spells, not other magical abilities.

**Cursebound 2** You take a –1 status penalty to saving throws against spells.

**Cursebound 3** Your weakness to spells is equal to your level.

**Cursebound 4** Your status penalty to saving throws against spells increases to –2.
name: Battle

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=14)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 135

Your mystery imparts an understanding of death and undeath in all their macabre complexity. You might have had a brush with death yourself—maybe even dying and returning to life—or carry the touch of undeath in your blood. If you commune with deities, you might speak with guardians of death like [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) and the [psychopomp ushers](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) (the most powerful among creatures that guide souls through the afterlife); a bringer of death like the [Mantis God Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=29) or the [Horseman of the Apocalypse Charon](/Deities.aspx?ID=135); or a herald of undeath such as [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548)
- **2nd** [False Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523)
- **3rd** [Ghostly Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1544)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Soul Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2072)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Armor of Bones](/Spells.aspx?ID=2073)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Claim Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=2074)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=70), [Decay](/Domains.aspx?ID=42), [Undeath](/Domains.aspx?ID=97), [Vigil](/Domains.aspx?ID=55)

**Oracle Feat** [Nudge the Scales](/Feats.aspx?ID=6055)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Living Death</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

Your body is slowly decaying even though you are alive, and using your powers furthers this unnatural living death. You carry a touch of the grave about you, manifesting as bloodless pallor, a faint smell of earth, or deathly cold skin.

**Cursebound 1** You gain weakness 2 to [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) and [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage. You can be targeted by and are hurt by both vitality and void damage even if one or the other normally has no effect on you (such as being targeted and damaged by heal even if you aren't undead). Any [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) or [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) you have to vitality or void is suppressed.

**Cursebound 2** You take a –1 status penalty to Fortitude saves.

**Cursebound 3** Your weakness to vitality and void damage is equal to 5 + your level.

**Cursebound 4** Your status penalty to Fortitude saving throws increases to –2.
name: Bones

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Cosmos](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=15)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 136

Celestial bodies great and small exert influence on you, giving you sublime cosmic power. Perhaps you see the glittering stars as a divine blessing, or perhaps you feel drawn to the infinitely dark spaces between. You might uphold deities like [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), or the deific lovers [Shizuru](/Deities.aspx?ID=315) and [Tsukiyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=317) who represent the sun and the moon— or you might draw power from dark entities from beyond the stars, like certain [Outer Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13), or destructive gods of the night like [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296) or the rat goddess [Lao Shu Po](/Deities.aspx?ID=312).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585)
- **1st** [Dizzying Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500)
- **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480)
- **5th** [Moon Frenzy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1609)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Spray of Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=2075)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Interstellar Void](/Spells.aspx?ID=2076)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Moonlight Bridge](/Spells.aspx?ID=2077)

**Related Domains** [Darkness](/Domains.aspx?ID=69), [Moon](/Domains.aspx?ID=84), [Star](/Domains.aspx?ID=52), [Nothingness](/Domains.aspx?ID=114)

**Oracle Feat** [Oracular Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=6056)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of the Sky's Call</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

Your body is drawn toward the heavens, making you lighter and less substantial than you should be. Your eyes glow with starry light, and your hair and clothing float and drift around you. When you have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) condition, you are [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) with a value equal to your cursebound value, and you take a status penalty to saves and DCs against all forms of [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) equal to your cursebound value.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Flames](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=16)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 136

Fire lives at the center of the world, the center of the sun, and the center of civilization. You might revere this elemental force, siphon power from the [Elemental Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31), or venerate a collection of deities such as [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), the Tian goddess of disasters and volcanoes [Lady Nanbyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=311), or the elemental lord of fire [Ymeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=108).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565)

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Engulfing Flames</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

Fires flare noticeably (though not dangerously) in your presence, you occasionally smoke slightly, and your body is almost painfully hot to the touch. When you have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) condition, you catch fire, taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your cursebound value, and any [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) or [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) you have to fire is suppressed. The flames shed [light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401) like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760), and if you enter an environment where they could not burn (such as underwater), you simply seethe with flameless heat. The flames subside when you begin [Refocusing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) to assuage your curse or if you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), but they resume if your Refocus activity is interrupted or when you return to consciousness.

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Life](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=17)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 137

The never-ending flow of life force within living beings is palpable to you. You might uphold the sanctity of life, or perhaps you seek to undermine it. You might draw power from the collective vitality of the world’s living creatures, hold some connection to [Creation’s Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27), or revere a collection of deities including [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286), [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), and the god of medicine [Qi Zhong](/Deities.aspx?ID=314).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744)
- **1st** [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678)
- **2nd** [False Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523)
- **5th** [Grisly Growths](/Spells.aspx?ID=1998)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Life Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=2081)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Delay Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=2082)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Life-Giving Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=2083)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=70), [Healing](/Domains.aspx?ID=78), [Pain](/Domains.aspx?ID=87), [Soul](/Domains.aspx?ID=51)

**Oracle Feat** [Nudge the Scales](/Feats.aspx?ID=6055)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Outpouring Life</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

Life energy flows outward from you and connects you to all living things, but you expend your vital essence to do so. Your presence comforts the ill and injured, causes scars to fade slightly, spurs new growth in plants, and otherwise infuses your surroundings with vitality. As your life force seeps outward, it becomes more difficult to keep your body functioning. Magical effects that restore Hit Points to you take a status penalty equal to your level (minimum 1) times your [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) value to the number of HP you recover.

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Lore](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=18)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 137

Knowledge and information come freely to you. You might use this lore to gain power or perhaps to understand the divine mysteries of the multiverse. You might have a conduit to the fabled Akashic Record, maintain a subtle telepathic connection to the collective subconscious of all living creatures, or follow in the footsteps of deities such as [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286), [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=288), Irori’s scholarly nephew [Gruhastha](/Deities.aspx?ID=39), the fey triune goddess of fate [Magdh](/Deities.aspx?ID=97), or the aeon god figure known as [the Monad](/Deities.aspx?ID=141).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)
and one [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill of your choice.

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- **1st** [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603)
- **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563)
- **6th** [Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Brain Drain](/Spells.aspx?ID=2084)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Access Lore](/Spells.aspx?ID=2085)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Dread Secret](/Spells.aspx?ID=2086)

**Related Domains** [Knowledge](/Domains.aspx?ID=80), [Magic](/Domains.aspx?ID=82), [Secrecy](/Domains.aspx?ID=91), [Truth](/Domains.aspx?ID=95)

**Oracle Feat** [Whispers of Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=6057)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Torrential Knowledge</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

You have a link to true divine knowledge, but your mortal mind struggles to process and act on what you know. Loose materials around you, such as dust, grains of rice, and droplets of water, slowly shift to form strange runes or faint, indecipherable writing, and you sometimes speak unintelligible truths or statements in unknown languages without realizing it. You take a status penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks and Will saving throws equal to your [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) value due to the torrential distractions of unasked-for knowledge flooding your mind. If you are cursebound 4, you additionally can’t speak, use [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) effects, or otherwise communicate with your allies. You can still cast spells, but you’re [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1.

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Tempest](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=19)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 137

The fury of the wind and waves pounds in your heart, whether your power flows from natural storms, a conduit to the elemental Planes of [Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29) and [Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=32), or through reverence of deities such as [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=284), the tengu god of storms [Hei Feng](/Deities.aspx?ID=307), the [pirate queen Besmara](/Deities.aspx?ID=32), or the [elemental lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) of [air](/Deities.aspx?ID=103) and [water](/Deities.aspx?ID=105).

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509)
- **1st** [Thunderstrike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721)
- **4th** [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562)
- **6th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Tempest Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=2087)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Thunderburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=2088)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Tempest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=2089)

**Related Domains** [Air](/Domains.aspx?ID=65), [Cold](/Domains.aspx?ID=41), [Lightning](/Domains.aspx?ID=47), [Water](/Domains.aspx?ID=98)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse of Inclement Headwinds</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=817" />
</traits>

The weather seems to always oppose you in ways large and small. Even when you are calm and at rest, your hair and clothing are inconveniently blown about by gentle winds, you are slightly damp from the faintest drizzle, and your touch often comes with a static shock. When you have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) condition, you are opposed by the elements, with the following effects.

**Cursebound 1** Lightning is drawn to you. You gain [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 and electricity spells or effects that have additional effects for a creature wearing or holding metal treat you as though you were wearing metal. Any [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) or [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) you have to such spells and effects is suppressed.

**Cursebound 2** Blowing winds impose a -2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls you make.

**Cursebound 3** Your weakness to electricity is equal to 5 + your level.

**Cursebound 4** The raging winds push you back, imposing a -10- foot status penalty to all your Speeds.

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Ash](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=20)</title>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 284

You see all things in the world as fleeting and temporary, waiting to be purified into their base essence: the ash left behind after a burning fire. While you understand fire as a necessary part of this process, you see it mostly as a tool to achieve final purity, not the true goal. You have much in common with oracles with the [flames](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=16) mystery, but you may consider them to be shortsighted, or at best, simply lacking in understanding of the truths that their burning fires impart.

You might see ash as the only true representation of the impermanent nature of life and its pointless existence. Alternatively, you may see the benefit ash can have on soils and farmlands as part of a larger cycle of death and rebirth. Perhaps you see the [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137), with its life cycle of being reduced to ash and reborn, as a sacred creature that has achieved a higher state of being and is worthy of imitation, or perhaps you see it as a tragic creature that can never reach the goal of perfection. If you follow a deity, you might follow gods of fire like [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), those who burn impurities to ash such as [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=33) or [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=294), or those who strive to burn all things to ash, such as the archdevil [Moloch](/Deities.aspx?ID=70) or the demon lord Flauros.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565)
- **1st** [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457)
- **2nd** [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606)
- **6th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Ashen Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1242)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Incendiary Ashes](/Spells.aspx?ID=1243)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Ash Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1241)

**Related Domains** [Destruction](/Domains.aspx?ID=71), [Dust](/Domains.aspx?ID=107), [Fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=76), [Nothingness](/Domains.aspx?ID=114)

**Oracle Feat** [Whispers of Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=6057)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse Of Creeping Ashes</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=319" />
</traits>

Your body is slowly being consumed by the fires of your internal power, purifying you with each passing day. You are occasionally wracked with dry, wheezing coughs, and wherever you go you leave behind a fine trace of ash that falls from your body. 

**Cursebound 1** You gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 to fire damage. Any [immunity or resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) you have to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) is suppressed. 

**Cursebound 2** Swirling ash imposes a -2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls you make.

**Cursebound 3** Your weakness to fire damage is equal to 5 + your level.

**Cursebound 4** You take a –10-foot status penalty to all your Speeds as your limbs begin to crumble like ash.
name: Ash

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Blight](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=21)</title>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 285

A spring bud will wither, a mighty oak will crash to the forest floor, and a callow youth will grow into maturity, then decline into old age. You know none of these things are cause for alarm or sadness. Decay is an essential part of nature’s cycle, as the old pass away to make room for, or to nourish, the new. After all, the flower must fall for the seed to grow, and the toppled tree not only makes room for new saplings to flourish, but also forms a home for many types of flora, fauna, and fungi. Disease, poison, and rot—these are all tools to dispose of creatures that have come to the end of their usefulness that rest in your hands. You might wield them to bring an end to lingering suffering or to spread contagion over once-thriving lands. Well-meaning doctors, persistent healers, or zealous adventurers may try to stand in your way, but blight will spread in the end. 

If you follow a deity, you might work toward the sinister ends of Rider of Disease [Apollyon](/Deities.aspx?ID=134), the demon lord [Cyth- V’sug](/Deities.aspx?ID=74), or the parasitic [Ghlaunder](/Deities.aspx?ID=36). You might find allies among followers of [Groetus](/Deities.aspx?ID=38) or the cults of the [Outer Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13). If you see your calling as part of the cycle of existence, you might venerate [monitor demigods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=12) such as [Mother Vulture](/Deities.aspx?ID=142) or commune with nature.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461)
- **1st** [Noxious Vapors](/Spells.aspx?ID=2011)
- **2nd** [Fungal Infestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1993)
- **5th** [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Ulcerous Canker](/Spells.aspx?ID=2426)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Purging Toxins](/Spells.aspx?ID=2427)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Accelerated Decomposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=2428)

**Related Domains** [Death](/Domains.aspx?ID=70), [Decay](/Domains.aspx?ID=104), [Disorientation](/Domains.aspx?ID=105), [Plague](/Domains.aspx?ID=115)

**Oracle Feat** [Whispers of Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=6057)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse Of Inevitable Rot</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=319" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

Your magic is a constant reminder that all creatures will eventually submit to the ravages of time and the environment. You are suffused with an aura of decay. Leaves wilt and insects fall out of the sky as you pass, silverware tarnishes in your hands, and the dregs of wine turn to vinegar in your goblet. 

**Cursebound 1** You gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage. 

**Cursebound 2** You take a –1 status penalty to saving throws against [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) and poisons. In addition, anyone attempting to [Treat your Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=2397), [Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=2398), or [Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) takes a –1 status penalty to the associated skill check. 

**Cursebound 3** Your weakness to acid and poison damage increases to twice your level. 

**Cursebound 4** Your status penalty to saving throws against diseases and poisons and the status penalty for anyone attempting to Treat your Disease, Poison, or Wounds increases to –2.
name: Blight

<title level="1" right="Oracle Mystery " pfs="Standard">[Time](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=22)</title>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 286, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 188

Whether by accident, fate, or purposeful meddling, you exist slightly outside of time. While initially unmoored by moments, the countless march of milliseconds builds up to drift you further and further out of sync with your natural timeline, often in contradictory directions. You might view this as a blessing, perhaps as a sign you’re somehow beyond mortality or you can’t be contained within reality’s limitations, or you might view it as a cruel curse that causes your life to slip by adrift even as you gain power over time. Many oracles of time are without faith and either come to rely upon their own instincts to survive or turn to strange rituals and forbidden magic in attempts to break free of time’s grasp. Those oracles who worship deities most often venerate [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), goddess of fate who sees all possible futures for all who ever lived, or [Alseta](/Deities.aspx?ID=30), goddess of transitions and the passage through time.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mystery Skill**
[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)

**Granted Spells**
- **Cantrip** [Time Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1193)
- **1st** [Déjà Vu](/Spells.aspx?ID=1986)
- **3rd** [Time Pocket](/Spells.aspx?ID=1192)
- **7th** [Time Beacon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2371)

**Initial Revelation Spell** [Temporal Distortion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1195)

**Advanced Revelation Spell** [Time Skip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1196)

**Greater Revelation Spell** [Manifold Lives](/Spells.aspx?ID=1197)

**Related Domains** [Change](/Domains.aspx?ID=102), [Fate](/Domains.aspx?ID=75), [Nothingness](/Domains.aspx?ID=114), [Time](/Domains.aspx?ID=121)

**Oracle Feat** [Oracular Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=6056)

</column>

<title level="2" right="Oracular Curse" pfs="Standard">Curse Of Turbulent Moments</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=319" />
</traits>

Time passes differently for you than for everyone else. You experience fluxes that might manifest as minor day-to-day changes in your apparent age, such as a gray lock of hair or a disappearing wrinkle, or as occasional ghostly temporal afterimages that slightly trail your movement. As you become more affected by your curse, time’s passage becomes more disjointed for you, making it difficult for you to react to the quick actions of others. You take a status penalty to your AC against attacks made against you from reactions or free actions and a status penalty to saving throws against effects that would make you [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) equal to your cursebound value.
name: Time

</traits>

Time passes differently for you than for everyone else. You experience fluxes that might manifest as minor day-to-day changes in your apparent age, such as a gray lock of hair or a disappearing wrinkle, or as occasional ghostly temporal afterimages that slightly trail your movement. As you become more affected by your curse, time's passage becomes more disjointed for you, making it difficult for you to react to the quick actions of others. You take a status penalty to your AC against attacks made against you from reactions or free actions and a status penalty to saving throws against effects that would make you [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) equal to your cursebound value.
name: Time
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 188, [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 286
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
Whether by accident, fate, or purposeful meddling, you exist slightly outside of time.
</summary>
spell_markdown: [Déjà Vu](/Spells.aspx?ID=1986), [Manifold Lives](/Spells.aspx?ID=2431), [Temporal Distortion](/Spells.aspx?ID=2429), [Time Beacon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2371), [Time Pocket](/Spells.aspx?ID=2610), [Time Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=2611), [Time Skip](/Spells.aspx?ID=2430)
summary_markdown: Whether by accident, fate, or purposeful meddling, you exist slightly outside of time.

<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Bands Of Imprisonment](/Ikons.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 43

**Usage** worn anklets, bracers, or circlet (often a headband)

</column>

---

These weighted bands don’t enhance your power—rather, they keep your strength in check, honing your discipline.

**Immanence** The _bands of imprisonment tighten_, keeping your mind sharp. You gain a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage equal to half your level.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Break Free;     **Effect** You can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) with a +2 status bonus on your check, then [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice your Speed in a straight line, and finally make a melee Strike. If you don’t need to Escape or you can’t move or choose not to, you still take the other actions listed.
name: Bands Of Imprisonment

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 43

**Usage** melee weapon that deals slashing or piercing damage

</column>

---

This blade subtly rattles in its scabbard, as if it wants to be unsheathed so it can consume violence.

**Immanence** The _barrow’s edge_ deals 1 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die to a creature it Strikes. If the creature is below half its maximum Hit Points, the weapon deals 3 additional spirit damage per weapon damage dice instead. _Barrow’s edge_ rattles more strongly when an enemy below half its maximum Hit Points is within the weapon’s reach to let you know a foe is verging closer to death.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509))Drink of my Foes;    **Requirements** Your last action was a successful Strike with the _barrow’s edge_; **Effect** Your blade glows as it absorbs your foe’s vitality. You regain Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt.
name: Barrow's Edge

It must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or be pulled directly toward you into a square adjacent to you.

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** imbued on the face

</column>

---

A fetching beauty spot under an eye or a smile as warm as the sun distracts foes and captures hearts alike.

**Immanence** Your beauty becomes supernaturally enhanced, distracting foes and imposing a –1 circumstance penalty to melee attack rolls against you.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))Captivating Charm;     **Effect** You focus your attention on a creature within 30 feet, overwhelming its senses. The creature must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by you until the start of your next turn. The condition ends if you use a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) action against the target, but not if you use a hostile action against its allies.
name: Eye-Catching Spot

</column>

---

Your eyes glint with an almost-tangible sharpness, letting you spot the tiniest swallow on the horizon or the swiftest arrow in flight.

**Immanence** Your vision sharpens and allows you to sense an enemy’s attack almost as soon as it begins, granting you a +1 status bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks and a +2 status bonus to your AC against ranged attacks.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))A Moment Unending;     **Effect** You take in every movement around you, affording you unparalleled accuracy. Your next successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against an enemy before the end of your next turn deals an additional 1d6 precision damage. This damage increases to 2d6 at 10th level and 3d6 at 18th level.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Flowing Spirit Strike;     **Effect** Make two Strikes with the gleaming blade, each against the same target and using your current multiple attack penalty. If the gleaming blade doesn’t have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait, the second Strike takes a –2 penalty. If both attacks hit, you combine their damage, which is all dealt as spirit damage. You add any precision damage only once. Combine the damage from both Strikes and apply [resistances and weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty.

Tattooed fists, savage claws, or even powerful gauntlets—you swing each with the fury of an animal from the woods.

**Immanence** [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with your hands of the wildling deal an additional 1 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage per weapon damage die. You are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to this splash damage.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Feral Swing;     **Effect** You lash out with both arms, rending all before you. Each creature in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) must succeed at a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC or take spirit damage equal to your normal Strike damage with your hands of the wildling. You can choose to swing with abandon, which imposes a –2 circumstance bonus to enemies’ saving throws, but causes you to become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Horn Of Plenty](/Ikons.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** a wallet, bag, or similar container of 1 Bulk you can wear on your body

</column>

---

Whether a bag, gourd, wallet, cornucopia, or similar food receptacle, this ikon recalls the harvest and hearth. The receptacle can store up to 1 Bulk of potions and elixirs, but no other items. The receptacle can’t be opened except by the ikon’s immanence and transcendence abilities.

Each day during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), the ikon produces one temporary [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308). You can choose to have it make a different [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=589) or [potion](/Traits.aspx?ID=672) you know the formula for. The level of any elixir or potion created by the horn must be your level or lower. The number of elixirs the horn creates increases to two at 8th level and three at 16th level, and you can choose each item individually. These temporary items vanish the next time you make your daily preparations, and any remaining effects of the temporary items end. A temporary elixir or potion has no value.

**Immanence** The _horn of plenty_ shimmers, allowing access to the stored consumables inside. You can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw a consumable and drink it in a single action while your divine spark rests within the horn. Other creatures can’t access the contents unless you allow them to.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Feed the Masses;     **Effect** The _horn of plenty_ allows you to transfer the effects of potions and elixirs to your allies. You Interact to draw a consumable from the horn and then Interact to drink it. Rather than nourishing yourself, the item’s effects are transferred to a willing ally within 60 feet, as if they had consumed it themself. If the consumable restores Hit Points, you can choose to divide the amount it restores between you and the ally freely (after rolling dice to determine the amount).
name: Horn Of Plenty

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Raise the Walls;     **Effect** You [Raise](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) the _mirrored aegis_, which summons ethereal shields that surround you and one ally of your choice within 15 feet in a tortoise shield formation. You and the ally gain a +1 status bonus to AC, Reflex saves, and any save against a force, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) effect for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

This animal hide, whether worn about the shoulders or waist, is all you need to survive even the harshest elements. When you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), choose [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. **Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Survive the Wilds;     **Effect** You wrap the pelt around yourself. You can choose to change the damage type the pelt is attuned to. The pelt shines gold, drawing the offending energies into itself. Until the start of your next turn, this shine creates an aura in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). You and all allies in the emanation gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against effects with that trait.
