markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Aldori Swordlords](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128

Aldori swordlords are masters of combat trained in the Aldori dueling sword. They value honor above everything.

- **1st Level or Higher**: Remove the creature's melee weapons (if any) from its items. Add the [Aldori dueling sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=88) to the creature's items with a Strike at the same bonus as its best melee Strike.
- **4th Level or Higher**: Add [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) to the creature's skills. The creature gains the [Duelist's Edge](/Feats.aspx?ID=878) free action.
- **7th Level or Higher**: Remove one of the creature's reactions (if any) from its available options, and replace it with the [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3) reaction.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Unnerving Prowess](/Feats.aspx?ID=880) free action.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Saving Slash](/Feats.aspx?ID=881) reaction.
name: Aldori Swordlords
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Aspis Consortium](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128

Members of the Aspis Consortium are always seeking ways to earn more coin. They are unscrupulous and aggressive in their methods, and put finances above morality.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) trait. Remove the [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [rogue's surprise attack class feature](/Classes.aspx?ID=10).
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) once per day as an innate arcane spell.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [rogue's deny advantage class feature](/Classes.aspx?ID=10).
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains 9th-level [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) as a constant innate arcane spell, which also affects the creature's items.
name: Aspis Consortium
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Bellflower Network](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128

Members of the Bellflower Network fight against slavery where they can, liberating slaves and helping those in need.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25) trait. Remove the [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) and [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) traits.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) to the creature's skills, with a bonus 2 higher when used to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).
- **4th Level or Higher**: Increase the creature's Speed by 5 feet.
- **7th Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to the creature's skills. It gains the [Unfettered Halfling](/Feats.aspx?ID=58) ancestry feat, even if it isn't a halfling.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) once per day as an innate divine spell.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Foil Senses](/Feats.aspx?ID=785) and [Slippery Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=842) skill feats.
name: Bellflower Network
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Eagle Knights](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128

The Eagle Knights are a military group dedicated to fighting tyranny and defending their homeland, Andoran. They risk their lives to fight injustice and champion the rights of the individual.

- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the [inspire competence](/Feats.aspx?ID=185) and [_inspire courage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=386) composition cantrips once per day each as innate divine spells.
- **7th Level or Higher**: When using the [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) reaction, if the creature rolls a success, it gets a critical success instead.
- **12th Level or Higher**: Remove one of the creature's reactions (if any) from its available options, and replace it with the [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3) reaction.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 9th-level [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) once per day as an innate divine spell.
name: Eagle Knights
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 128
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Firebrands](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

Firebrands tend to be rebellious liberators or boastful daredevils. They seek to draw attention to themselves.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25) trait. Remove the [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: Increase the creature's Speed by 5 feet.
- **7th Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) to the creature's skills. If you added Deception, add the [Confabulator](/Feats.aspx?ID=769) skill feat; if you added Diplomacy, add the [Glad-Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=787) feat; and if you added Intimidation, add the [Battle Cry](/Feats.aspx?ID=759) feat.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 3rd-level [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) once per day as an innate occult spell.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Bravo's Determination](/Feats.aspx?ID=1070) feat.
name: Firebrands
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Hellknights](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

Hellknights are disciplined individuals who seek to maintain order and quell chaos. The myriad Hellknight orders are all highly structured and regimented. This template gives a low-level creature significantly more treasure than the normal maximum for its level, due to expensive armor; adjust the total treasure in your adventure accordingly.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) trait. Remove the [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Remove the creature's armor (if any) from its items. Add [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=11) to the creature's items. If the creature wasn't wearing heavy armor before this adjustment, its Speed is reduced by 5 feet and it gains +1 AC.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature gains resistance to mental and slashing equal to half its level.
- **7th Level or Higher**: Add [Hellknight plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=14) to the creature's items instead of splint mail. The creature can cast [_discern lies_](/Spells.aspx?ID=74) once per day as an innate divine spell.
- **12th Level or Higher**: Remove one of the creature's reactions (if any) from its available options, and replace it with [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3). If applying the 1st-level template ability caused the creature's Speed to be reduced, that Speed is now restored to its original value.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature's attacks deal 1d6 additional lawful damage on a hit. As is normal for aligned damage, this damages only chaotic creatures.
name: Hellknights
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Houses of Perfection](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

The students of the Houses of Perfection are dedicated to the martial and mystical practices of Vudra. They are disciplined figures capable of improbable physical feats.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) trait. Remove the [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Remove the creature's melee weapons (if any) from its items. Replace the creature's melee weapon attacks with a [fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=1) unarmed attack that deals d8s for damage.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [_ki strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=486) ki spell and gains a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. The creature's ki spells are divine.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature's Speed increases by 5 feet. It gains the [_perfect strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=542) ki spell. Its focus pool increases to 2 Focus Points.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature adds the appropriate ki spell for its School of Perfection: [_unblinking flame revelation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=543) for Unblinking Flame, [_unbreaking wave advance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=544) for Unbreaking Waves, [_unfolding wind rush_](/Spells.aspx?ID=545) for Unfolding Wind, or [_untwisting iron buffer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=546) for Untwisting Iron. Its focus pool increases to 3 Focus Points.
- **17th Level or Higher**: Choose one saving throw. When the creature rolls a success on that saving throw, it gets a critical success instead.
name: Houses of Perfection

<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Knights of Lastwall](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

Though their nation of Lastwall has fallen, the Knights of Lastwall remain vigilant against the undead. They are unwavering in their cause and fight with honor.

- **1st Level or Higher**: Remove the creature's melee weapons (if any) from its items. Add a [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31) and [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) to the creature's items with a Strike at the same attack bonus. It gains the [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32) reaction. Add [Undead Lore](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Reactive Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=360) fighter feat.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Necromantic Resistance](/Feats.aspx?ID=884) feat.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Lastwall Warden](/Feats.aspx?ID=887) feat.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the [_champion's sacrifice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=393) devotion spell once per day as an innate divine spell.
name: Knights of Lastwall

<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Lion Blades](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=9)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

The Lion Blades are a secret organization in Taldor, protecting the nation via espionage, intrigue, and infiltration. They creatively use every tool at their disposal.

- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: Observers are never granted circumstance bonuses to Perception checks due to the creature [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a person of a different ancestry, age, and so on, as long as the disguise is appropriate for its size.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Lost in the Crowd](/Feats.aspx?ID=918) feat.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains a +10-foot status bonus to its Speeds.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Flicker](/Feats.aspx?ID=922) feat.
name: Lion Blades
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 129

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Pathfinder Society](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=11)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130

The Pathfinder Society recruits members of all kinds, so long as they are willing to report, explore, and cooperate. These like-minded adventurers and explorers travel the world to seek new knowledge and experiences.

- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Pathfinder Society Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), one other Lore, and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) to the creature's skills.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature adds its level to its ability modifier to determine its total modifier for any skill not listed in the creature's stat block.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains a _[wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470)_ and the [Careful Explorer](/Feats.aspx?ID=872) feat.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) once per day as an innate occult spell.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 9th-level [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) once per day as an innate occult spell.
name: Pathfinder Society
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Red Mantis Assassin](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=12)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130

The Red Mantis are assassins for hire who work under a strict code of rules and requirements. Once they are on the job, these tireless killers relentlessly pursue their targets.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) and [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) traits. Remove the [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25) and [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) traits.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Remove the creature's melee weapons from its items. Add two [sawtooth sabers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=65) to the creature's items, and add a sawtooth saber Strike with an attack bonus equal to the creature's highest melee Strike.
- **4th Level or Higher**: Add [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) to the creature's skills.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Crimson Shroud](/Feats.aspx?ID=898) feat.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Mantis Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=899) feat.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 9th-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) once per day as an innate divine spell.
name: Red Mantis Assassin
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Whispering Way](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=13)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130

The Whispering Way is a cult of necromancers, nihilists, and undead intent on supporting Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant. They are zealous in their efforts to further the lich's goals.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) trait. Remove the [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Undead Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) to the creature's skills. It can cast [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=35) as an innate divine cantrip.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the [_undeath's blessing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=518) bloodline spell once per day as an innate divine spell.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 2nd-level [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) once per day as an innate divine spell; for every 2 levels it has beyond 7th, _harm_ is heightened another spell level.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Command Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=277) cleric class feat. It gains an additional use of its [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) innate spell that it can use only with Command Undead.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) ability, which means the creature is damaged by positive effects that damage undead, is healed by negative effects that heal undead, and doesn't benefit from [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effects.
name: Whispering Way

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Deity Worshiper](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=14)</title>

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 10

Theme templates allow a GM to replicate creatures and NPCs that worship specific deities.

- **All Creatures**: Add any traits in the deity's alignment. Remove any alignment traits not in the deity's alignment.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add the deity's favored weapon and a [wooden religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of the deity to the creature's items. Add a favored weapon Strike with an attack bonus equal to the creature's highest melee or ranged Strike (whichever is appropriate). If the favored weapon is simple, increase the damage die of that Strike by one step.
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the initial domain spell of one of the deity's domains and gains a pool of 1 Focus Point.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the 1st-level spell the deity grants [clerics](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) three times per day as a divine innate spell.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast the advanced domain spell of the domain you chose for 4th level or higher, and its focus pool increases to 2 Focus Points.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature has been truly blessed. Either the creature can cast the deity's other deity spells of up to 7th level once per day each as divine innate spells, or, at the GM's discretion, the creature gains the benefits of one of the deity's boons.
name: Deity Worshiper

<additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[The Magaambya](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=15)</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 130

The Magaambya is an academic institution dedicated to the study of magic, and its members are known for combining magical traditions in unique ways.

- **All Creatures**: Add the [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) trait. Remove the [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) trait.
- **1st Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) and [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) as innate cantrips. Decide whether these are arcane or primal cantrips when applying the template.
- **4th Level or Higher**: Add [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) and [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22) to the creature's skills. It gains the [Recognize Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=834) skill feat.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains either 2nd-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) as an innate arcane spell or 2nd-level [_entangle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) as an innate primal spell. The spell can be cast once per day.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [Quickened Casting](/Feats.aspx?ID=199) wizard class feat.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 7th-level [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) once per day as an innate arcane spell and 7th-level [_chain lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) once per day as an innate primal spell.
name: The Magaambya

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Hellbound Creature](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=16)</title>

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 64

Sadly, there's no shortage of people willing to trade all they have for the promise of power. Infernal contracts are the most common of such agreements, but powerful devils can offer even greater abilities at the cost of the mortal's soul and an obligation to advance Hell's infernal agenda. This mortal becomes a fiend known as a Hellbound creature.

Hellbound is a theme template applied to creatures and NPCs to reflect powers gained through a bargain with [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18). Theme templates provide additional abilities to the base creature based on its level. When you add any template that grants numerous additional abilities, you should strongly consider removing one or more of the creature's original abilities to compensate, or consider raising the creature's level by 1 and adjusting its other statistics accordingly.

- **All Creatures**: Change the creature's alignment to evil, if it isn't already. Add the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=44) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) traits. The creature gains resistance to fire equal to its level and weakness to good equal to half its level.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to the creature's skills. The creature gains [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12).

- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 1st-level [_burning hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=30) once per day as a divine innate spell; for every 2 levels it has beyond 4th, _burning hands_ is heightened an additional spell level.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [infernal pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=23) ritual.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) once per day as a divine innate spell.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) once per day as a divine innate spell.
name: Hellbound Creature
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 64
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Creature Theme Template">[Hellbound Creatures](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=17)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 98

Sadly, there’s no shortage of people willing to trade all they have for the promise of power. [Infernal contracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=1460) are the most common of such agreements, but powerful devils can offer even greater abilities at the cost of the mortal’s soul and an obligation to advance Hell’s infernal agenda. This mortal becomes a fiend known as a Hellbound creature.

<title level="2" right="">Creating a Hellbound Creature</title> Hellbound is a theme template applied to creatures and NPCs to reflect powers gained through a bargain with Hell. Theme templates provide additional abilities to the base creature based on its level. When you add any template that grants numerous additional abilities, you should strongly consider removing one or more of the creature’s original abilities to compensate, or consider raising the creature’s level by 1 and adjusting its other statistics accordingly.

- **All Creatures**: The creature gains the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait. If the creature had the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait, it loses it. Add the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) traits. The creature gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to fire equal to its level and [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to holy equal to half its level.
- **1st Level or Higher**: Add [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to the creature's skills. The creature gains [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410).
- **4th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast 1st-rank [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) once per day as a [divine ](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901); for every 2 levels it has beyond 4th, _breathe fire_ is heightened an additional spell rank.
- **7th Level or Higher**: The creature gains the [_diabolic pact_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) ritual.
- **12th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) once per day as a divine innate spell.
- **17th Level or Higher**: The creature can cast [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) once per day as a divine innate spell.
name: Hellbound Creatures

<additional-info>
**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 98

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 86 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The [_wyrmblessed_](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=17) sorcerer bloodline provides options for primal and imperial dragons. Character with a similar draconic connection, such as draconic bloodline sorcerors or draconic instinct barbarians, can also select from these dragon types. You must own a copy of Lost Omens: The Mwangi Expanse to select these options._ You summon the fury of a mighty [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) and manifest incredible abilities. Perhaps your culture reveres draconic majesty, or you gained your connection by drinking or bathing in dragon's blood or after watching a marauding wyrm burn your village. Raging Resistance You resist piercing damage and the damage type of your dragon’s breath weapon.

<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Standard">[Fury](/Instincts.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 87

Your rage comes from a deep and purely personal well within you. You use your rage as you choose.

<title level="2" >Anathema and Instinct Ability</title>
You don't have an anathema or an instinct ability. Instead, you gain an additional 1st-level [barbarian feat](/Feats.aspx?Traits=18).

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
Increase the additional damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 2 to 6. If you have greater weapon specialization, instead increase the additional damage from Rage to 12.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist physical weapon damage, but not physical damage from other sources (such as unarmed attacks).
name: Fury

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Standard">[Giant](/Instincts.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 87

Your rage gives you the raw power and size of a [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=79). This doesn't necessarily mean you revere giants—you might scoff at them or even aspire to slay them! You could instead seem like a giant to other people due to your exceptional strength or larger-than-life emotions and ego.

<title level="2" >Anathema</title>
Failing to face a personal challenge of strength is anathema.

<title level="2" >Titan Mauler (Instinct Ability)</title>
You can use a weapon built for a Large creature if you are Small or Medium (both normally and when raging). If you're not Small or Medium, you can use a weapon built for a creature one size larger than you. You gain access to this larger weapon, which can be of any weapon type otherwise available at character creation. It has the normal Price and Bulk for a [weapon of its size](/Rules.aspx?ID=258). When wielding such a weapon in combat, increase your additional damage from Rage from 2 to 6, but you have the clumsy 1 condition because of the weapon's unwieldy size. You can't remove this clumsy condition or ignore its penalties by any means while wielding the weapon.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
Increase the damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) when using a larger weapon from 6 to 10; if you have greater weapon specialization, increase it from 10 to 18.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist bludgeoning damage and your choice of cold, electricity, or fire, chosen when you gain raging resistance.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Standard">[Spirit](/Instincts.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 87

Whether you are emotionally sensitive to the spirits around you; worship ancestors or apparitions; or are haunted by the specter of an ancestor, relative, friend, or foe, your rage takes the form of a spiritual possession.

<title level="2" >Anathema</title>
Disrespecting corpses or spirits is anathema to your instinct; defending yourself against [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures is not.

<title level="2" >Spirit Rage (Instinct Ability)</title>
While raging, you can increase the additional damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 2 to 3 and change its damage type to negative or positive, instead of the damage type for your weapon or unarmed attack (choose each time you Rage). If you choose to deal negative or positive damage, your weapon or unarmed attack gains the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune, which makes it more effective against incorporeal creatures, and your Rage action gains the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) traits, plus [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), as appropriate.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
When using spirit rage, increase the damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 3 to 7. If you have greater weapon specialization, instead increase the damage when using spirit rage to 13.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist negative damage, as well as damage dealt by the attacks and abilities of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures, regardless of the damage type.
name: Spirit

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Standard">[Superstition](/Instincts.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 108

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Given the slightly relaxed rules around edicts and anathema in Society play, a barbarian with the superstition instinct can benefit from spells and magic items if they are an unavoidable part of a Society adventure, such as if a scenario assumes the PCs are transported to their mission location via a teleport spell, or if an adventure requires that all PCs participate in a magical ritual._

A deep distrust of magic drives you to forgo and counter the metaphysical nonsense of spellcasters. Whether you're a member of a superstitious family or culture that distrusts magic, a warrior in constant battle against wizards and witches, a survivor of a magical accident that instilled an intense aversion in your mind and body, or a scion of a bloodline known for its magic resistance, your rage is inimical to magic. This makes you an excellent mage hunter but slow to trust practitioners of magical arts. Your extremely restrictive anathema grants you powers beyond those of other instincts.

<title level="2" >Anathema</title>
Willingly accepting the effects of magic spells (including from scrolls, wands, and the like), even from your allies, is anathema to your instinct. You can still drink potions and invest and activate most magic items you find, though items that cast spells are subject to the same restrictions as all other spells. If an ally insists on using magic on you despite your unwillingness, and you have no reason to believe they will stop, continuing to travel with that ally of your own free will counts as willingly accepting their spells (as do similar circumstances) and thus is also anathema to your instinct.

<title level="2" >Superstitious Resilience (Instinct Ability)</title>
While raging, you gain a +2 status bonus to all saves against magic. Increase your damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 2 to 4 against creatures that can cast spells. Once every 10 minutes, when you Rage, you regain Hit Points equal to the temporary Hit Points you gain from that Rage action.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
Increase the damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 4 to 8 against creatures that can cast spells. If you have greater raging specialization, instead increase the damage from Rage to 12 against creatures with spells and 8 against other creatures.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
Choose two associated magical traditions: arcane and occult, arcane and primal, divine and occult, or divine and primal. The resistance from your raging resistance class feature applies against all damage you take from spells cast with these two traditions of magic, regardless of the type of damage dealt by the spell.
name: Superstition

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 54 This instinct option for barbarians allows you to channel elemental forces, bonding with a single element and its associated plane. Perhaps your ancestors were elemental conjurers or blessed by an elemental lord. Select an element from the Elemental Instincts table to be your instinct's element. If your element offers multiple damage types, choose one of those type when you select your element. ## Elemental Instincts
**Element****Trait****Damage**
AirAirElectricity or slashing
EarthEarthBludgeoning or piercing
FireFireFire
MetalMetalPiercing or slashing
WaterWaterBludgeoning or cold
WoodWoodBludgeoning or piercing
 Anathema Disrespecting an elemental creature is anathema to your instinct; defending yourself against one is not. Purposefully despoiling the elemental plane associated with your element is anathema to your instinct, though this doesn't prevent you from responsibly altering that plane. Elemental Rage (Instinct Ability) While raging, you're cloaked in a vortex of elemental matter; you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) against ranged attacks. You can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63). While raging, you increase the additional damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 2 to 4 and change its damage type to the one you selected for your element.
If you have any [kineticist](/Classes.aspx?ID=23) [impulses](/Traits.aspx?ID=502) with the same element type as the one you chose for your instinct, such as ones gained by taking the [Kineticist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4333) multiclass feat, you can use them while raging even if they have the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. Specialization Ability When you use elemental rage, you increase the damage from [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) from 4 to 6. If you have greater weapon specialization, instead increase the damage from Rage when using elemental rage from 6 to 12. Raging Resistance You resist the damage dealt by attacks and abilities of elemental creatures of your chosen element, as well as creatures made of your element, regardless of the damage type. You also resist damage dealt by attacks, spells, and abilities with your elemental trait.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 75

Your rage gives you the raw power and size of a giant. This doesn’t necessarily mean you revere giants—you might scoff at them or even aspire to slay them! You could instead seem like a giant to other people due to your exceptional strength or larger-than-life emotions and ego.

<title level="2" >Instinct Ability—Titan Mauler</title>
You can use a weapon built for a Large creature if you are Small or Medium (both normally and when raging). If you’re not Small or Medium, you can use a weapon built for a creature one size larger than you. You start with one such weapon, which you receive for free. It must be a common melee or ranged weapon, it must have a Price of 9 gp or less (not including the Price adjustment for being a larger weapon), and it must be common or you must otherwise have access to it. It is your personal weapon and has no value if sold unless you later add runes to it, and it has the normal Bulk for a weapon of its [size](/Rules.aspx?ID=2163).

When wielding a larger weapon in combat, increase your additional damage from Rage from 2 to 6, but you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 condition because of the weapon’s unwieldy size. You can’t remove this clumsy condition or ignore its penalties by any means while wielding the weapon.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
Increase the damage from Rage when using a larger weapon from 6 to 10; if you have greater weapon specialization, increase it from 10 to 18.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist bludgeoning damage and your choice of cold, electricity, or fire, chosen when you gain raging resistance.

A deep distrust of magic drives you to forgo and counter the metaphysical nonsense of spellcasters. <title level="2" >Instinct Ability—Superstitious Resilience</title>
Your deep superstition means it’s anathema for you to learn or [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), or to wield or use an item that can be activated to Cast a Spell. You cannot reduce your frightened condition below 1 as long as you are affected by the spell or effect.

<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Limited">[Decay](/Instincts.aspx?ID=15)</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 70

Your rage is intrinsically tied to the decomposition of organic beings, and the new growth that decay allows. You may have meddled with ancient relics created by Ghorus, been infected by exposure to [Ayrzul’s Blight](/Diseases.aspx?ID=41) or the Tanglebriar, or been blessed by the green man [Zibik](/Deities.aspx?ID=685). As you unleash your rage, you rot, becoming riddled with mold and flesh-eating [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614). Though this rot recedes when you let your emotions settle, it doesn’t go away completely, and the scars left behind spread bit by bit throughout your lifetime, multiplying with every rage.

<title level="2" >Anathema</title>
Preventing the decomposition of dead creatures or plants is anathema to your instinct, as is destroying or interfering with the growth of molds or fungi in a natural setting. This doesn’t prevent you from defending yourself against fungus creatures or hazards or harvesting fungi or molds solely for sustenance.

<title level="2" >Instinct Ability-Rotting Rage </title>
When you rage, you can choose to increase the additional damage from Rage from 2 to 6 and change its damage type to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), instead of the damage type for your weapon or unarmed attack. If you do this, your Rage action gains the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) and poison traits, and you take 1 damage at the end of each of your turns as your flesh decays. This damage can’t be reduced or avoided by any means. You may make this choice only when your rage begins, and it remains in effect until your rage ends.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Specialization Ability</title>
When you use rotting rage, increase the additional damage from Rage from 6 to 10, but the damage you take at the end of your turn increases to 5. If you have greater weapon specialization, instead increase the damage from Rage when using rotting rage from 10 to 18, but the damage you take increases to 10.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist poison damage, as well as damage dealt by the attacks and abilities of creatures with the fungus trait, regardless of the damage type.
name: Decay

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Barbarian Instinct " pfs="Limited">[Ligneous](/Instincts.aspx?ID=16)</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 71

Your rage is powered by the steady growth of the natural world, from the blossoming of flowers to the cultivation of vegetables and the towering, imposing might of the grandest trees. You might be descended from powerful [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) or [dryads](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3112), been exposed to powerful [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) magic, or been transformed by the resurgence of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). As you unleash your rage, your flesh becomes covered in woody growths, which spread like bark across your body.

<title level="2" >Anathema</title>
Despoiling nature and killing plants unnecessarily is anathema to your instinct. Defending yourself against [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures or [hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=2576) is not.

<title level="2" >Instinct Ability-Wooden Rage</title>
While raging, you can increase the additional damage from Rage from 2 to 6. If you do this, reduce your Speed by 10 feet, as the bark plates covering your body are a hinderance to mobility. This speed reduction can’t be overcome by any means, though it can be offset by speed increases.

<title level="2" >Specialization</title>
When you use wooden rage, increase the additional damage from Rage from 6 to 10. If you have greater weapon specialization, instead increase the damage from Rage when using wooden rage from 10 to 18.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Raging Resistance</title>
You resist piercing and slashing damage, but you gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to fire equal to 3 + your Constitution modifier, as your bark-like flesh is highly flammable.
name: Ligneous

</traits>

<additional-info>

</additional-info>

<summary>
Whether guildmaster or cutpurse, you covet something, or many things, that don’t belong to you, and you’re driven to ever greater heights of larceny. …
</summary>
summary_markdown: Whether guildmaster or cutpurse, you covet something, or many things, that don’t belong to you, and you’re driven to ever greater heights of larceny. …

anathema: knowingly aid in any plot to end the world, mock or deride those who predict the end of the world
edict: help to prevent (or at least forestall) doomsday by keeping allies (and their gear) in good health (or repair), console those who are despondent
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Doomsayer's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 17

As long as people have dwelt in the world, there have been those who foresaw, feared, or worked toward that world’s end. You know all things must end and have long felt that at some point in your life, you would stand on the precipice of Golarion’s final fate—not to usher it along, but to deny the agents of this doomsday the satisfaction of seeing your world end. Until then, you’ll do your best to keep the world and your allies in shape to face these ultimate threats. When you attempt a check to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384), [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396), or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) (but not when you use [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=5125)), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Repair or Administer First Aid, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** help to prevent (or at least forestall) doomsday by keeping allies (and their gear) in good health (or repair), console those who are despondent

**Anathema** knowingly aid in any plot to end the world, mock or deride those who predict the end of the world
name: Doomsayer's Calling

anathema: deny a creature (including yourself) the chance to sleep long enough to dream, deride those who have ambitions that they’re unlikely to ever achieve
edict: share your dreams with your friends and take interest if they share their dreams with you, take inspiration from your dreams when you create

<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Dreamer's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 17

Your dreams have always felt more real than reality itself, and upon learning of the Dimension of Dreams you weren’t surprised—indeed, you’ve often found yourself entering these Dreamlands when you sleep. You’ve long felt that something in the Dreamlands has been calling to you, and that some great rescue or revelation awaits in a dream you’ve yet to experience. In the meantime, your dreams remain a potent source of inspiration and power, and the ability to draw from them to affect the waking world is something you’ve always been able to do. When you attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367), or when you attempt a check to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) any work of art, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at a check to Craft or at a Recall Knowledge check about a subject associated with dreaming or the Dreamlands, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** share your dreams with your friends and take interest if they share their dreams with you, take inspiration from your dreams when you create

**Anathema** deny a creature (including yourself) the chance to sleep long enough to dream, deride those who have ambitions that they’re unlikely to ever achieve
name: Dreamer's Calling

anathema: willingly destroy ancient texts or other forms of written knowledge without first preserving that lore in some way, treat another’s convictions or beliefs as fantasy or delusion
edict: seek out rare magic spells and items from ancient Thassilon, learn knowledge about Thassilon that has been forgotten

<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Runelord's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 18

Even before Karzoug rose, you felt something tugging to you from beyond. Strange memories, sudden insights, vivid dreams, or wild hunches that lead to discoveries or unexpected successes have been the norm for you. Once knowledge of the legacies of Thassilon began to spread anew into the modern world after Karzoug rose from slumber, you’ve increasingly come to realize these visions and inspirations come to you from the past—maybe even one of your past lives—and that something from the time before Earthfall in ancient Thassilon is linked to your soul. When you attempt a check to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366), or cast a ritual, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check to Identify Magic, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** seek out rare magic spells and items from ancient Thassilon, learn knowledge about Thassilon that has been forgotten

**Anathema** willingly destroy ancient texts or other forms of written knowledge without first preserving that lore in some way, treat another’s convictions or beliefs as fantasy or delusion
name: Runelord's Calling

anathema: allow those in the Saga Lands who need aid to suffer if there’s something you can do to help them, regard your own saga as more important than anyone else’s, since your tale has yet to be fully told—and not even you know its ending
edict: do what you can to protect the people of the Saga Lands, preserve legends and myths in writing or other forms of art for future generations to enjoy and learn from
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Saga's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=16)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 18

The Saga Lands do not come by that appellation lightly, for the storied legacies of this region predate Earthfall and have roots in worlds far beyond Golarion. Whether you were born in these lands or drawn there, those storied legacies have resonated with you on a supernatural level. It seems like each time you learn of some new legend or tale of this land passed down through the generations, there’s something in it that directly applies to your life. You’ve taken to relying upon these legends—be they ancient or modern—as guideposts for your own life’s course, knowing full well that they’re calling to you and that you’re destined to be the focus of one yourself.

When you attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check or a check to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at a check to Gather Information, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** do what you can to protect the people of the Saga Lands, preserve legends and myths in writing or other forms of art for future generations to enjoy and learn from

**Anathema** allow those in the Saga Lands who need aid to suffer if there’s something you can do to help them, regard your own saga as more important than anyone else’s, since your tale has yet to be fully told—and not even you know its ending
name: Saga's Calling

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Chain](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

**Favored Weapon**
[Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Chain are skilled at hunting down and capturing criminals, especially those who dare flee from justice.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Shackles of Law
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

You attempt to subdue a target with an attack and a follow-up grab. Make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Grapple the creature you hit. If you’re wielding a flail, you can ignore Grapple’s requirement that you have a hand free. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after you’ve made both of them.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Sturdy Bindings
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

Your grapples are incredibly reliable. When you roll a critical failure on a check to Grapple a target, you get a failure instead. In addition, when a creature you have grabbed rolls a failure on its check to Escape, it gets a critical failure instead, and if it rolls a critical success, it gets a success instead.
name: Order of the Chain

<additional-info>

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Gate](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

**Favored Weapon**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

The Hellknight signifers of the Order of the Gate seek to prevent lawlessness of all types through the use of their considerable spellcasting prowess and command of fiendish outsiders.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Locate Lawbreakers
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

Your pursuit is relentless, and you are able to magically locate stolen objects and outlaws. You gain [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) as an innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. If you are a member of the Order of the Gate, when you reach 14th level, the spell is heightened to 5th level.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Devil Allies
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You can call in devils to assist your efforts. You gain [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) as an innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. When you cast this spell, you can summon only creatures that have the devil trait. At 16th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened by 1 level (to a maximum of 8th level when you reach 20th level).
name: Order of the Gate

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Godclaw](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=3)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Favored Weapon**
[Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched by others who enforce the law.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Dedication to the Five
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

Your dedication to the Godclaw borders on a cleric’s. You gain the cleric’s [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=265) feat but must select [perfection](/Domains.aspx?ID=26), [protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=27), [tyranny](/Domains.aspx?ID=33), or [zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=37) as your domain. In addition, you can use any religious symbol of [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=1), [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10), or [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) as your divine focus.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Blessing of the Five
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

You call out to the Godclaw to heal your allies. You cast the 3-action version of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), heightened to a level 1 lower than half your level rounded up. You can select up to four creatures in the area to remain unaffected by the spell. You can instead use this ability to bring a creature within 30 feet that has died within the last round back from the dead. If you do, the creature is restored to 1 Hit Point and is doomed 1 for 24 hours.
name: Order of the Godclaw

<additional-info>

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Nail](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Favored Weapons**
[Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26), [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=28)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Nail see civilized society as an ideal and seek to unite all people within their cultural baseline. They also seek to destroy anyone who does not accept Chelaxian culture along with the wild beasts that threaten the safety of civilization.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Trailblazing Stride
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You are used to moving through the wilderness. While moving on land, you ignore the effects of non-magical difficult terrain.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Reveal Beasts
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You are adept at revealing—or creating—weaknesses among those who are a threat to civilization. If you roll a success on a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify an animal or a beast, you get a critical success instead. The next time you damage that creature, it gains weakness 5 during that attack to one damage type of your choice that you dealt it.
name: Order of the Nail

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Pyre](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Favored Weapon**
[Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)

</column>

The Order of the Pyre is dedicated to eliminating those faiths and beliefs they consider to be a threat to the Inner Sea region. They are particularly keen on stamping out dangerous cults.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Righteous Resistance
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You can cast [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) twice per day as a divine innate spell, except you can target only yourself. If you grant yourself resistance to fire damage, the resistance is equal to either the spell’s normal value or to your level, whichever is higher.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Spiritual Disruption
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You channel disruptive power that severs a foe’s spiritual connection to their heretical magic. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, any time the target attempts to cast a divine or occult spell, the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) unless they succeed at a DC 5 flat check (DC 7 on a critical hit); if the target is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), it must attempt the harder of the two flat checks (but not both). This effect lasts for 1 minute.
name: Order of the Pyre

<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Rack](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Favored Weapons**
[Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Rack seek to eliminate wastefulness throughout the Inner Sea region. They seek to quash pointless dreams, quell rebellions, and destroy dangerous inventions.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Disillusionment
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

Dreams are pointless illusions you seek to quash. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an illusion or dream effect or a Perception check to disbelieve an illusion, you get a critical success instead. If you roll a critical failure on any of those checks, you get a failure instead.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Silence Heresy
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You channel a power that stifles dangerous words. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, the target is unable to speak in a voice louder than a whisper for 1 round. This prevents the target from using most auditory effects involving its voice, and it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or the spell is disrupted. If your Strike is a critical hit, the target is entirely unable to speak for 1 round and is limited to a whisper for 1 minute thereafter. Once a creature has been affected by this ability, it is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
name: Order of the Rack

<column gap="tiny">

**Favored Weapons**
[Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7), [Scourge](/Weapons.aspx?ID=89), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

Hellknights of the Order of the Scourge hunt those who pervert the law for their own selfish ends. They seek to destroy corruption and those who hide behind unjust laws and bureaucracy.

    right="Lesser Order Benefit"

</title>

</traits>

</column>

When you roll a success on a save against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. In addition, any time you gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition, reduce the condition’s value by 1. As usual, this ability reduces the initial [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition by 1, so if you have multiple similar abilities, they don’t both take effect.

With a moment spent focusing, you can locate the telltale signs of corruption no matter what means it may use to hide from your sight. For 1 minute, you can see through up to 5 feet of stone, wood, or similar barriers as if they didn’t exist (though any amount of metal or denser barriers block this effect).

The creed of the Order of the Chain is “All men lift themselves up upon the backs of others.” These Hellknights seek to punish those who actively impede what they believe to be civilization’s progress.

    level="2"

</traits>

([flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606)) You attempt to subdue a target with an attack and a follow-up grab. Make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an Athletics check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the creature you hit. If you're wielding a flail or spiked chain, you can ignore Grapple's requirement that you have a hand free, and if you succeed at the Athletics check, you get a critical success instead. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both of them.

In addition, when a creature you have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) rolls a failure on its check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), it gets a critical failure instead, and if it rolls a critical success, it gets a success instead.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</column>

Your dedication to the Godclaw borders on a cleric's. You gain the cleric's [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4644) feat but must select [duty](/Domains.aspx?ID=108), [might](/Domains.aspx?ID=83), [perfection](/Domains.aspx?ID=89), or [protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=90) as your domain. Whenever you cast the focus spell gained from this feat, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's rank that last for 1 round. In addition, you can use any religious symbol of [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277), [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286), or [Uirch](/Deities.aspx?ID=560) as your divine focus.

once per day

You call out to the Godclaw to heal your allies. You cast the 3-action version of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), heightened to a rank one lower than half your level rounded up. You can select up to five creatures in the area to remain unaffected by the spell. You can instead use this ability to bring a creature within 30 feet that has died within the last round back from the dead. If you do, the creature is restored to 1 Hit Point and is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.

<title level="1" right="Animistic Practice " pfs="Standard">[Liturgist](/Practices.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 14

You draw forth your apparitions through the power of song and dance, connecting the spiritual to the physical. These performances can be of your own creation or follow the specific rites of your religion.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Song of Invocation</title>
You can sing out to the spirits to have them spin and twirl around you. You gain the [Circle of Spirits](/Feats.aspx?ID=7121) feat.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Dancing Invocation</title>
The movement of your body grants power to your magic. When you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), Step, or [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), you also Sustain an apparition spell or vessel spell.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Invocation of Praise</title>
Your liturgies call to the spirits even in the heat of battle. When you roll initiative, you can use Circle of Spirits as a free action. If your first action on your first turn is to cast an apparition cantrip granted by your primary apparition, you reduce the number of actions it takes to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).
name: Liturgist

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 15 You are particularly good at acting as a conduit for spiritual energy and tend to associate more freely with a wide array of apparitions, though you tend not to form the deep bond with a single apparition that other animists often develop. Invocation of Unity The lines between your body and your apparition are blurry. You gain the [Relinquish Control](/Feats.aspx?ID=7122) feat. Dual Invocation You can build powerful bonds with multiple apparitions. You can select two of your attuned apparitions to be your primary apparitions. If you have the supreme incarnation class feature, you choose which apparition's avatar form you manifest each time you cast the [_avatar_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1446) spell. The number of Focus Points in your focus pool is equal to the number of focus spells you have or the number of primary apparitions you are attuned to, whichever is higher (maximum 3). Invocation of Synchronization The line between your intentions and those of the apparition possessing you blurs, leaving you better able to support each other in the throes of combat. While you have Relinquished Control to an apparition, you gain the following additional benefits:
  • Once per round, you can attempt a Recall Knowledge check as a free action.
  • All Strikes you make with a weapon or unarmed attack deal an additional 2 points of spirit damage.
  • You gain resistance equal to half your level against all physical damage, but have weakness equal to your level to spirit damage.
name: Medium

<title level="1" right="Animistic Practice " pfs="Standard">[Seer](/Practices.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 15

You are particularly sensitive to the presence and influence of [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). You can detect lingering spirits, offering you some defense against them.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Invocation of Sight</title>
You shift your eyes easily to the spirit world, intuiting the needs of apparitions and other spiritual entities based on how they appear to you. You gain the [Apparition Sense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7120) feat. You also gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and AC against the effects of [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) and the abilities of spirits and [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) undead.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Invocation of Protection</title>
Your status as an intermediary across planar boundaries grants you further defenses against spiritual ailments. You gain spirit resistance and void resistance equal to half your level, and your status bonus to saving throws and AC against the effects of haunts and the abilities of spirits and incorporeal undead increases to +2.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Invocation of Resilience</title>
You are a bulwark against the terrible spirits that lurk in the dark. Your apparition sight becomes a precise sense and your status bonus to saving throws and AC against the effects of haunts and the abilities of spirits and incorporeal undead increases to +3.
name: Seer

<title level="1" right="Animistic Practice " pfs="Standard">[Shaman](/Practices.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 16

You form close bonds with your apparitions that allow you to invest them with the rare ability to take on a material form and directly affect the physical world.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Invocation of Embodiment</title>
You allow your apparition to inhabit a physical form. You gain the [Spirit Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7123) feat. At 2nd level, you gain the [Enhanced Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=4717) feat.

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Invocation of Growth</title>
Your bond with the physical form of your chosen apparition grows stronger. You gain the [Incredible Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=5004) feat.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Invocation of Otherworldliness</title>
Your apparition familiar becomes ever more powerful, straddling the line between physical and spiritual. Your spirit familiar can take a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait to become incorporeal for 1 minute. It gains the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) trait, meaning it can move through physical creatures and such creatures can move through it. It can't attempt Strength-based checks (such as [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376)) against corporeal creatures, and corporeal creatures can't attempt such checks against it. It gains immunity to precision damage and resistance to physical damage (except damage from Strikes with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune) equal to half your level (double against non-magical damage) but weakness to force damage equal to half your level. However, when incorporeal, your spirit familiar can travel no more than 60 feet from you and must maintain line of effect to you; this typically prevents it from moving through walls. Your spirit familiar can Dismiss this effect.
name: Shaman
